# LLMtxt Documentation
> SDK and API reference for llmtxt — context sharing for AI agents

## Links
- Web App: https://www.llmtxt.my
- API: https://api.llmtxt.my
- Docs: https://docs.llmtxt.my
- Full context: https://docs.llmtxt.my/llms-full.txt
- GitHub: https://github.com/kryptobaseddev/llmtxt

---

# API Specification (api.llmtxt.my)

# LLMtxt API Specification

## Overview
LLMtxt is a text document sharing service optimized for LLM agents.
Store documents, share short URLs, and query only the parts you need.
Progressive disclosure dramatically reduces token costs: instead of
reading a 10,000-line document, query just the 50 lines you need.

## Base URL
https://api.llmtxt.my

## Web Interface
https://www.llmtxt.my

## Authentication
No authentication required for basic usage.
Rate limits: 100 requests/minute per IP.

## Quick Start for Agents

1. Store a document:
   POST /compress {"content": "..."}
   → Returns {"slug": "abc123", "url": "..."}

2. See what's inside (without reading it all):
   GET /documents/abc123/overview
   → Returns structure, sections, token count

3. Query only what you need:
   GET /documents/abc123/lines?start=1&end=50
   GET /documents/abc123/search?q=authentication
   GET /documents/abc123/query?path=$.users[0]
   GET /documents/abc123/sections/Authentication

4. Get raw content (plain text, no JSON wrapper):
   GET /documents/abc123/raw

## Token Savings Headers
All progressive disclosure endpoints return these headers:
- X-Token-Count: tokens in the returned content
- X-Total-Tokens: tokens in the full document
- X-Tokens-Saved: tokens you did NOT need to read

## Endpoints

### Create Document
POST /compress
Content-Type: application/json

Request:
{
  "content": "string (required) - Document content",
  "format": "string (optional) - 'json' or 'text', auto-detected if omitted",
  "schema": "string (optional) - 'prompt-v1' for OpenAI/Anthropic validation"
}

Response (201):
{
  "slug": "string - 8-character document ID",
  "url": "string - Full URL to document",
  "format": "string - 'json' or 'text'",
  "tokenCount": "number - Estimated token count",
  "compressionRatio": "number - Compression efficiency",
  "originalSize": "number - Size in bytes before compression",
  "compressedSize": "number - Size in bytes after compression"
}

### Retrieve Full Document
POST /decompress
Content-Type: application/json

Request:
{
  "slug": "string (required) - Document slug"
}

Response (200):
{
  "slug": "string",
  "content": "string - Original content",
  "format": "string",
  "tokenCount": "number",
  "createdAt": "number - Unix timestamp"
}

### Get Document Metadata
GET /documents/:slug

Response (200):
{
  "slug": "string",
  "format": "string",
  "tokenCount": "number",
  "compressionRatio": "number",
  "originalSize": "number",
  "compressedSize": "number",
  "createdAt": "number",
  "accessCount": "number"
}

## Progressive Disclosure Endpoints

These endpoints let agents read only what they need from a document.

### Document Overview
GET /documents/:slug/overview

Returns document structure without the full content. Use this first
to understand what's in a document before drilling in.

Response (200):
{
  "slug": "string",
  "format": "json | markdown | code | text",
  "lineCount": "number",
  "tokenCount": "number",
  "sections": [
    {
      "title": "string - Section name",
      "depth": "number - Nesting level (0 = top)",
      "startLine": "number - 1-indexed",
      "endLine": "number - 1-indexed inclusive",
      "tokenCount": "number",
      "type": "heading | json-key | code-block | function | class"
    }
  ],
  "keys": "array (JSON only) - Top-level keys with types and previews",
  "toc": "array (Markdown only) - Table of contents"
}

### Line Range
GET /documents/:slug/lines?start=1&end=50

Fetch specific lines from the document.

Query params:
- start: 1-indexed start line (default: 1)
- end: 1-indexed end line, inclusive (default: 50)

Response (200):
{
  "slug": "string",
  "startLine": "number",
  "endLine": "number",
  "content": "string",
  "tokenCount": "number - Tokens in returned content",
  "totalLines": "number - Total lines in document",
  "totalTokens": "number - Total tokens in document",
  "tokensSaved": "number - Tokens NOT sent"
}

### Search
GET /documents/:slug/search?q=auth&context=2&max=20

Search within a document. Supports plain text and /regex/ patterns.

Query params:
- q: search query (string or /regex/flags)
- context: lines of context around matches (default: 2, max: 10)
- max: maximum results (default: 20, max: 100)

Response (200):
{
  "slug": "string",
  "query": "string",
  "resultCount": "number",
  "tokenCount": "number",
  "totalTokens": "number",
  "tokensSaved": "number",
  "results": [
    {
      "line": "number - 1-indexed line number",
      "content": "string - Matching line",
      "contextBefore": "string[] - Lines before match",
      "contextAfter": "string[] - Lines after match"
    }
  ]
}

### JSONPath Query (JSON documents)
GET /documents/:slug/query?path=$.users[0].name

Query JSON documents using JSONPath syntax.
Supports: $.key, $.nested.path, $.array[0], $.array[*]

Query params:
- path: JSONPath expression

Response (200):
{
  "slug": "string",
  "path": "string",
  "result": "any - The queried value",
  "tokenCount": "number",
  "totalTokens": "number",
  "tokensSaved": "number"
}

Error (400):
{
  "error": "Query failed",
  "message": "string - What went wrong (e.g., key not found, index out of bounds)"
}

### List Sections
GET /documents/:slug/sections

List all detected sections in the document.
Sections are auto-detected based on format:
- Markdown: headers (##)
- JSON: top-level keys
- Code: functions, classes
- Text: paragraphs

Response (200):
{
  "slug": "string",
  "format": "string",
  "sections": [{ "title", "depth", "startLine", "endLine", "tokenCount", "type" }]
}

### Get Section by Name
GET /documents/:slug/sections/:name

Extract a specific section by name.

Response (200):
{
  "slug": "string",
  "section": { "title", "depth", "startLine", "endLine", "tokenCount", "type" },
  "content": "string - Section content",
  "tokenCount": "number",
  "totalTokens": "number",
  "tokensSaved": "number"
}

Error (404):
{
  "error": "Section not found",
  "availableSections": ["string - List of section names"]
}

### Raw Content
GET /documents/:slug/raw
GET /documents/:slug/raw?start=1&end=50

Returns plain text content (no JSON wrapper). Optional line range.
Content-Type: text/plain

## Schemas

### prompt-v1
Standard LLM prompt format (OpenAI/Anthropic compatible)

Structure:
{
  "system": "string (optional) - System prompt",
  "messages": [
    {
      "role": "'system' | 'user' | 'assistant'",
      "content": "string"
    }
  ],
  "temperature": "number (optional) - 0-2",
  "max_tokens": "number (optional) - Positive integer"
}

### List Schemas
GET /schemas

## Error Handling

All errors return JSON:
{
  "error": "string - Error type",
  "message": "string - Description",
  "details": "array (optional) - Validation errors"
}

Status codes: 200, 201, 400, 404, 429, 500

## Example: Progressive Disclosure Workflow

```bash
# 1. Agent A stores a large config file
curl -X POST https://api.llmtxt.my/compress \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"content": "{\"database\":{\"host\":\"db.example.com\"},\"users\":[...]}"}'
# → {"slug": "abc123", "tokenCount": 5000}

# 2. Agent A shares the slug with Agent B

# 3. Agent B checks what's in the document (costs ~50 tokens instead of 5000)
curl https://api.llmtxt.my/documents/abc123/overview
# → {"format": "json", "keys": [{"key":"database","type":"object{1}"},{"key":"users","type":"array[100]"}]}

# 4. Agent B queries only the database config (costs ~15 tokens)
curl "https://api.llmtxt.my/documents/abc123/query?path=\$.database"
# → {"result": {"host": "db.example.com"}, "tokensSaved": 4985}

# 5. Agent B searches for a specific user (costs ~30 tokens)
curl "https://api.llmtxt.my/documents/abc123/search?q=alice"
# → {"results": [...], "tokensSaved": 4970}
```

## Versioning Endpoints

### Update Document (Creates Version)
PUT /documents/:slug
Content-Type: application/json

Request:
{
  "content": "string (required) - Updated content"
}

Response (200):
{
  "slug": "string",
  "versionNumber": "number",
  "contentHash": "string",
  "tokenCount": "number"
}

### List Versions
GET /documents/:slug/versions

### Get Version Content
GET /documents/:slug/versions/:num

### Diff Between Versions
GET /documents/:slug/diff?from=1&to=3

Response (200):
{
  "fromVersion": "number",
  "toVersion": "number",
  "addedLines": "number",
  "removedLines": "number",
  "addedTokens": "number",
  "removedTokens": "number"
}

### Apply Patch
POST /documents/:slug/patch
Auth: Required (registered user)

Request:
{
  "patchText": "string (required) - Unified diff",
  "changelog": "string (required) - Change description"
}

## Lifecycle Endpoints

### Transition State
POST /documents/:slug/transition
Auth: Required (document owner)

Request:
{
  "state": "'REVIEW' | 'LOCKED' | 'ARCHIVED'",
  "reason": "string (optional)"
}

States: DRAFT → REVIEW → LOCKED → ARCHIVED
Only DRAFT and REVIEW documents accept patches.

### Approve Document
POST /documents/:slug/approve
Auth: Required (registered user)

Request:
{
  "comment": "string (optional)"
}

### Reject Document
POST /documents/:slug/reject
Auth: Required (registered user)

Request:
{
  "comment": "string (required)"
}

### List Approvals
GET /documents/:slug/approvals

### List Contributors
GET /documents/:slug/contributors

## Similarity Endpoint

### Find Similar Sections
GET /documents/:slug/similar?q=query&method=ngram&threshold=0

## Knowledge Graph Endpoint

### Extract Mentions/Tags/Directives
GET /documents/:slug/graph?topicLimit=10&agentLimit=10

## Retrieval Planning Endpoint

### Plan Token-Budget-Aware Retrieval
POST /documents/:slug/plan-retrieval

Request:
{
  "tokenBudget": "number (required)",
  "query": "string (optional)",
  "minScore": "number (optional, default 0.5)",
  "includeIntro": "boolean (optional, default true)"
}

## Table of Contents

### Lightweight TOC
GET /documents/:slug/toc

### Batch Section Query
POST /documents/:slug/batch

Request:
{
  "sections": ["string[] - Section names to retrieve"]
}

## Signed URLs

### Generate Signed URL
POST /signed-urls
Auth: Required (document owner)

Request:
{
  "slug": "string",
  "agentId": "string",
  "conversationId": "string",
  "expiresIn": "number (ms, default 3600000)"
}

## Authentication

### Sign Up
POST /auth/sign-up/email
{"email": "string", "password": "string", "name": "string (optional)"}

### Sign In
POST /auth/sign-in/email
{"email": "string", "password": "string"}

### Anonymous Session
POST /auth/sign-in/anonymous
{}

### Get Session
GET /auth/get-session

### Sign Out
POST /auth/sign-out

## Version
API Version: 2026.4.1
Last Updated: 2026-03-31

---

# SDK Reference (npm: llmtxt)

# llmtxt
> Primitives and SDK for LLM agent content workflows: compression, patching, progressive disclosure, signed URLs, collaborative document lifecycle, and retrieval planning
> Version: 2026.4.1

## Install

```bash
npm install -D llmtxt
```

## Documentation

- [API Reference](./api-reference.md): Full API documentation with signatures and examples
- [Full Context](./llms-full.txt): Complete symbol documentation for deep LLM consumption

## Packages

### main
84 functions, 71 types, 2 classes, 13 variables

- compress() — 
- decompress() — 
- encodeBase62() — 
- decodeBase62() — 
- generateId() — 
- hashContent() — 
- calculateTokens() — 
- calculateCompressionRatio() — 
- computeSignature() — 
- computeSignatureWithLength() — 
- computeOrgSignature() — 
- computeOrgSignatureWithLength() — 
- deriveSigningKey() — 
- createPatch() — 
- applyPatch() — 
- reconstructVersion() — 
- squashPatchesWasm() — 
- wasmTextSimilarity() — 
- wasmTextSimilarityNgram() — 
- isExpired() — 
- computeDiff() — 
- createClient() — 
- getLineRange() — Extract a range of lines from a document, returning content and token statistics.
- searchContent() — Search document content for lines matching a query string or regex.
- detectDocumentFormat() — Detect the structural format of a document using content heuristics.
- generateOverview() — Generate a structural overview of a document for progressive disclosure.
- queryJsonPath() — Execute a JSONPath-style query against JSON content.
- getSection() — Extract a named section from a document by title.
- extractMentions() — Extract  from message content.
- extractTags() — Extract #tags from message content.
- extractDirectives() — Extract /directives from message content.
- buildGraph() — Build a knowledge graph from an array of messages.  Nodes: agents (from fromAgentId + mentions), topics (#tags), decisions (/decision messages) Edges: mentions (agent→agent), discusses (agent→topic), decides (agent→decision), participates (agent→agent in same conversation)
- topTopics() — Find the most connected topics in the graph. Returns topics sorted by number of discussing agents.
- topAgents() — Find the most active agents in the graph. Returns agents sorted by total edge weight (mentions + discussions + decisions).
- isPredefinedSchema() — Type-guard that checks whether a string is a registered predefined schema name.
- getPredefinedSchema() — Retrieve a predefined Zod schema by name.
- validateJson() — Validate content as JSON, optionally against a predefined schema.
- validateText() — Validate content as plain text or markdown.
- detectFormat() — Auto-detect whether a string is JSON, markdown, or plain text.
- validateContent() — Validate content for a given format, with optional schema enforcement and content size limits.
- autoValidate() — Auto-detect the content format and then validate accordingly.
- computeSignature() — Compute the HMAC-SHA256 signature for signed URL parameters. Delegates to the Rust WASM module.
- computeSignatureWithLength() — Compute signature with configurable length. Use 16 for short-lived URLs (default), 32 for long-lived URLs (128 bits).
- generateSignedUrl() — Generate a signed URL for accessing a document.
- verifySignedUrl() — Verify a signed URL's signature and expiration. Uses timing-safe comparison to prevent timing attacks.
- computeOrgSignature() — Compute the HMAC-SHA256 signature for org-scoped signed URL parameters. Includes orgId in the HMAC payload for organization-level access control. Returns 32 hex characters (128 bits) by default.
- computeOrgSignatureWithLength() — Compute org-scoped signature with configurable length.
- generateOrgSignedUrl() — Generate an org-scoped signed URL for accessing a document. The URL includes the org parameter for organization-level access verification.
- verifyOrgSignedUrl() — Verify an org-scoped signed URL's signature and expiration.
- generateTimedUrl() — Generate a signed URL that expires after the given duration.
- deriveSigningKey() — Derive a per-agent signing key from their API key. Delegates to the Rust WASM module.
- isExpired() — Check whether a timestamp has expired. Returns false for null/undefined (no expiration set).
- compressSnapshot() — Compress a session snapshot JSON string. Returns compressed data + metadata for storage/retrieval.
- decompressSnapshot() — Decompress a snapshot back to JSON string. Verifies integrity via content hash if provided.
- compressSessionData() — Compress a session snapshot object directly. Serializes to JSON, then compresses.
- decompressSessionData() — Decompress and parse a snapshot back to an object.
- snapshotSummary() — Generate a human-readable summary of a snapshot for handoff messages. Useful for agents posting session summaries to ClawMsgr.
- extractNgrams() — Extract character-level n-grams from text. Normalizes whitespace and lowercases before extraction.
- extractWordShingles() — Extract word-level n-grams (shingles) from text. Better for longer content where word order matters.
- jaccardSimilarity() — Jaccard similarity: |A ∩ B| / |A ∪ B|. Returns 0.0 (no overlap) to 1.0 (identical).
- textSimilarity() — Compute similarity between two texts using character n-grams. Returns 0.0 to 1.0.
- contentSimilarity() — Compute similarity using word shingles. Better for comparing messages or documents where word choice matters more than character patterns.
- minHashFingerprint() — Generate a compact fingerprint for content using MinHash. The fingerprint is an array of hash values that can be compared for approximate similarity without storing the full n-gram set.  Two fingerprints with many matching values indicate similar content.
- fingerprintSimilarity() — Estimate similarity between two MinHash fingerprints. Returns approximate Jaccard similarity (0.0 to 1.0).
- rankBySimilarity() — Rank a list of texts by similarity to a query. Returns indices sorted by descending similarity, with scores.
- isValidTransition() — Check whether a state transition is allowed.
- validateTransition() — Validate a proposed state transition with a detailed result.
- isEditable() — Check whether a document state allows content modifications.
- isTerminal() — Check whether a document state is terminal (no further transitions).
- reconstructVersion() — Reconstruct a document at a specific version by applying patches sequentially from the base content.
- validatePatchApplies() — Check whether a patch applies cleanly to the given content.
- squashPatches() — Squash a sequence of patches into a single unified diff.  Applies all patches sequentially to the base content, then produces one diff from base to final state.
- computeReversePatch() — Compute a reverse patch that undoes a version's changes.
- diffVersions() — Compute a diff summary between two versions of a document.
- evaluateApprovals() — Evaluate reviews against an approval policy.  Filters out stale and timed-out reviews, then checks whether the remaining approvals meet the policy threshold.
- markStaleReviews() — Mark reviews as stale when a document version changes.  Returns a new array with updated review statuses. Does not mutate input.
- inlineRef() — Create a content reference for inline storage.
- objectStoreRef() — Create a content reference for object-store storage.
- versionStorageKey() — Generate a storage key for a document version.  Convention: `attachments/{slug}/v{version}.zlib`
- shouldUseObjectStore() — Determine whether content should be stored in object-store vs inline.  Threshold: content larger than 64KB compressed goes to object-store.
- attributeVersion() — Compute attribution data for a single version change.
- buildContributorSummary() — Build aggregated contributor summaries from version attributions.
- planRetrieval() — Plan which sections to retrieve given a token budget.  When a query is provided, sections are ranked by text similarity to the query and packed greedily by descending score. Without a query, sections are packed in document order.
- estimateRetrievalCost() — Estimate the token cost of fetching specific sections.

- LRUCache — Generic least-recently-used (LRU) cache with time-to-live support.
- LlmtxtDocument — High-level document orchestration object.  Each instance wraps a single document slug and delegates all persistence to the provided StorageAdapter. All computation (diffing, hashing, disclosure, consensus) uses the pure SDK functions.

- CacheStats — Snapshot of cache performance statistics.
- LRUCacheOptions — Configuration options for constructing an `LRUCache` instance.
- DiffResult — 
- ContentFormat — Supported content formats.
- DocumentMode — Lifecycle state for collaborative documents.
- DocumentMeta — Metadata for a stored document.
- VersionMeta — Metadata for a single document version.
- VersionSummary — Summary of a version for listing (no content).
- VersionDiff — Result of comparing two versions.
- LlmtxtRef — Reference to an llmtxt document shared in a message.
- AttachmentOptions — Options for creating an attachment via the bridge.
- AttachmentAccessMode — Attachment fetch mode supported by the bridge/API layer.
- AttachmentSharingMode — Share state persisted by the API layer for an attachment.
- AttachmentReshareOptions — Options for re-sharing an existing attachment.
- AttachmentVersionOptions — Options for appending a version to an existing attachment slug.
- LlmtxtClientConfig — 
- UploadResult — 
- FetchResult — 
- ReshareResult — 
- ResignResult — Backward-compatible alias. Prefer `ReshareResult`.
- AttachmentVersionResult — 
- Section — A logical section identified within a document.
- DocumentOverview — High-level structural overview of a document.
- SearchResult — A single match returned by `searchContent`.
- LineRangeResult — Result of extracting a line range from a document via `getLineRange`.
- GraphNode — 
- GraphEdge — 
- KnowledgeGraph — 
- MessageInput — 
- PredefinedSchemaName — Union of all registered predefined schema name strings.
- JsonFormat — Inferred TypeScript type for any valid JSON value (object, array, primitive).
- TextFormat — Inferred TypeScript type for plain text content.
- MarkdownFormat — Inferred TypeScript type for markdown content (string alias).
- PromptV1 — Inferred TypeScript type for the standard LLM prompt format.
- PromptMessage — Inferred TypeScript type for a single prompt message with role and content.
- CompressRequest — Inferred TypeScript type for content compression request payloads.
- DecompressRequest — Inferred TypeScript type for content decompression request payloads.
- SearchRequest — Inferred TypeScript type for content search request payloads.
- ValidationResult — Outcome of a content validation operation.
- ValidationError — A single validation error with location, message, and error code.
- ValidateContentOptions — Options for content validation.
- SignedUrlParams — Parameters that uniquely identify a signed URL access grant.
- SignedUrlConfig — Configuration for generating and verifying signed URLs.
- VerifyResult — Outcome of verifying a signed URL.
- OrgSignedUrlParams — Parameters for org-scoped signed URLs (Phase 5 enterprise). Extends conversation-scoped params with an organization ID.
- SnapshotMeta — 
- CompressedSnapshot — 
- SnapshotOptions — 
- DocumentState — Lifecycle state of a collaborative document.
- StateTransition — Record of a lifecycle state change.
- TransitionResult — Result of attempting a state transition.
- VersionEntry — A single version entry in a document's patch stack.
- ReconstructionResult — Result of reconstructing a document at a specific version.
- PatchValidationResult — Result of validating whether a patch applies cleanly.
- VersionDiffSummary — Summary of changes between two versions.
- ApprovalStatus — Status of an individual review.
- Review — A single review from an agent.
- ApprovalPolicy — Policy governing how approvals are evaluated.
- ApprovalResult — Result of evaluating reviews against a policy.
- StorageType — How document content is stored.
- CompressionMethod — Compression method used for stored content.
- ContentRef — Reference to where a document's compressed content lives.  Inline: content is stored directly in the database (small documents). Object-store: content is stored in S3-compatible storage, referenced by key.
- StorageMetadata — Metadata about a stored document blob.
- StorageAdapter — Storage adapter that platforms implement.
- VersionAttribution — Attribution data for a single version.
- ContributorSummary — Aggregated contribution summary for a single agent.
- PlannedSection — A section selected for retrieval.
- RetrievalPlan — The output of retrieval planning.
- RetrievalOptions — Options for retrieval planning.
- CreateVersionOptions — Options for creating a new version.
- LlmtxtDocumentOptions — Options for constructing an LlmtxtDocument.

- jsonFormatSchema — Schema that accepts any valid JSON value.
- textFormatSchema — Schema that accepts any string value (plain text or markdown).
- markdownFormatSchema — Schema for markdown content, stored as a plain string.
- promptMessageSchema — Schema for a single message within an LLM prompt conversation.
- promptV1Schema — Schema for the standard LLM prompt format (OpenAI / Anthropic style).
- predefinedSchemas — Registry of predefined content schemas keyed by name.
- compressRequestSchema — Schema for incoming content compression requests.
- decompressRequestSchema — Schema for incoming content decompression requests.
- searchRequestSchema — Schema for incoming content search requests.
- DEFAULT_MAX_CONTENT_BYTES — Default maximum content size in bytes (5 MB).
- DEFAULT_MAX_LINE_BYTES — Default maximum line length in bytes (64 KB).
- DOCUMENT_STATES — All valid document states in lifecycle order.
- DEFAULT_APPROVAL_POLICY — Default approval policy: 1 approval, no timeout.

---

# Backend Internals (@llmtxt/backend)

# @llmtxt/backend
> API documentation for @llmtxt/backend
> Version: 1.0.0

## Install

```bash
npm install -D @llmtxt/backend
```

## Documentation

- [API Reference](./api-reference.md): Full API documentation with signatures and examples
- [Full Context](./llms-full.txt): Complete symbol documentation for deep LLM consumption

## Packages

### main
27 functions, 36 types, 34 variables

- getDocumentCacheKey() — Generate cache key for a document
- shouldSkipCache() — Check if cache should be skipped based on query params
- cacheDocumentContent() — Middleware to cache document content Usage: app.get('/documents/:slug', cacheDocumentContent, async (request, reply) =  ... )
- cacheDocumentMetadata() — Middleware to cache document metadata
- setCachedContent() — Store document content in cache
- setCachedMetadata() — Store document metadata in cache
- invalidateDocumentCache() — Invalidate cache for a document
- invalidateAllCache() — Invalidate all cache
- getCacheStats() — Get cache stats for both caches
- apiRoutes() — Register core document API routes: compress, decompress, validate, search, schemas, and cache management.
- disclosureRoutes() — Register progressive disclosure routes: overview, sections, toc, search, lines, raw, query, and batch endpoints for token-efficient content retrieval.
- versionRoutes() — Register version management routes: document update, version listing, version retrieval, and pairwise diff computation.
- authRoutes() — Register authentication routes by proxying all /auth/* requests to the better-auth handler.
- requireAuth() — Authenticate the request via session cookie. Populates request.user and request.session, or returns 401.
- requireRegistered() — Require an authenticated, non-anonymous user. Calls requireAuth first, then rejects anonymous sessions with 403.
- requireOwner() — Require the authenticated user to be the document owner. Checks slug from route params against document ownerId. Returns 403 if not owner, 404 if document not found.
- lifecycleRoutes() — Register lifecycle and consensus routes: state transitions, approve/reject voting, approval listing, and contributor attribution.
- patchRoutes() — Register patch route: POST /documents/:slug/patch to apply a unified diff and create a new version. Requires authentication and editable document state.
- similarityRoutes() — Register similarity route: GET /documents/:slug/similar?q=query&method=ngram&threshold=0 to rank document sections by similarity to a query.
- graphRoutes() — Register knowledge graph route: GET /documents/:slug/graph to extract mentions, #tags, and /directives from document content.
- retrievalRoutes() — Register retrieval planning route: POST /documents/:slug/plan-retrieval for token-budget-aware section selection. Ranks sections by relevance and greedily packs within a token budget.
- signedUrlRoutes() — Register signed URL route: POST /signed-urls to generate time-limited HMAC-signed access tokens for document retrieval. Requires owner authentication.
- getDocumentWithContent() — Fetch a document by slug, resolving content from cache or database with decompression.
- handleContentNegotiation() — Handle content negotiation for slug requests. Returns true if the request was handled (response sent), false otherwise.
- extractSlugWithExtension() — Check if a URL path looks like a document slug with an explicit extension. Supported extensions: .txt, .json, .md
- extractSlug() — Check if a URL path looks like a document slug. Slugs are short alphanumeric strings at the root level. Returns the slug if valid, null otherwise.
- renderViewHtml() — Render the server-side HTML view template for a document page, including meta tags and structured content display.

- User — 
- NewUser — 
- Session — 
- NewSession — 
- Document — 
- NewDocument — 
- Version — 
- NewVersion — 
- StateTransition — 
- NewStateTransition — 
- Approval — 
- NewApproval — 
- Contributor — 
- NewContributor — 
- SignedUrlToken — 
- NewSignedUrlToken — 
- VersionAttribution — 
- NewVersionAttribution — 
- InsertUser — 
- SelectUser — 
- InsertSession — 
- SelectSession — 
- InsertDocument — 
- SelectDocument — 
- InsertVersion — 
- SelectVersion — 
- InsertStateTransition — 
- SelectStateTransition — 
- InsertApproval — 
- SelectApproval — 
- InsertContributor — 
- SelectContributor — 
- InsertSignedUrlToken — 
- SelectSignedUrlToken — 
- InsertVersionAttribution — 
- SelectVersionAttribution — 

- users — Users table - supports both anonymous (24hr TTL) and registered accounts.  Anonymous users get a generated ID and no credentials. They are auto-purged after `expiresAt`. Registered users provide email/password and persist indefinitely until explicitly deleted.
- sessions — Sessions table - server-side session tokens for both user types.  Anonymous users get a session on first document creation. Registered users get a session on login. Sessions are invalidated on logout or expiration.
- accounts — Accounts table — better-auth manages OAuth and credential providers.
- verifications — Verifications table — better-auth email verification and password reset tokens.
- documents — Documents table - stores compressed text documents.  Extended with lifecycle state, ownership, anonymous flag, storage mode, version tracking, and approval policy to support the full SDK feature set (lifecycle, consensus, versioning, signed URLs).
- versions — Versions table - tracks document version history with patch support.  Extended with patchText for incremental storage (SDK VersionEntry), storage mode, and base version reference for patch chains.
- stateTransitions — State transitions table - audit trail for document lifecycle changes.  Maps directly to the SDK StateTransition interface. Every call to `LlmtxtDocument.transition()` inserts a row here.
- approvals — Approvals table - stores individual review/approval records.  Maps directly to the SDK Review interface from consensus.ts. Each row is one review action; the latest per reviewer wins.
- contributors — Contributors table - materialized aggregation of per-agent attribution.  Maps directly to the SDK ContributorSummary interface. Denormalized from version + attribution data for fast reads. Refreshed on each version creation via application logic.
- signedUrlTokens — Signed URL tokens table - persists generated signed URL grants.  Maps to the SDK SignedUrlParams interface. Each row represents an active access grant. Expired tokens are cleaned up by the purge job. Supports both conversation-scoped and org-scoped signatures.
- versionAttributions — Version attributions table - per-version diff metadata for attribution.  Maps directly to the SDK VersionAttribution interface. Stores the computed diff stats (lines/tokens added/removed, sections modified) for each version, enabling fast attribution queries without recomputing diffs.
- insertUserSchema — 
- selectUserSchema — 
- insertSessionSchema — 
- selectSessionSchema — 
- insertDocumentSchema — 
- selectDocumentSchema — 
- insertVersionSchema — 
- selectVersionSchema — 
- insertStateTransitionSchema — 
- selectStateTransitionSchema — 
- insertApprovalSchema — 
- selectApprovalSchema — 
- insertContributorSchema — 
- selectContributorSchema — 
- insertSignedUrlTokenSchema — 
- selectSignedUrlTokenSchema — 
- insertVersionAttributionSchema — 
- selectVersionAttributionSchema — 
- db — Drizzle ORM instance connected to the SQLite database with full schema.
- auth — Better-auth instance with email/password + anonymous authentication, cookie-based sessions, and 24hr anonymous user TTL.
- contentCache — LRU cache for decompressed document content strings, keyed by slug.
- metadataCache — LRU cache for document metadata objects, keyed by slug.
- publicDir — Absolute path to the public assets directory for static file serving.
